parameters.content:
Instagram Image Carousel Post – Product Collection Drop

Receives a product collection via webhook, validates slides (2–10), and builds a carousel caption. Each image is fetched, uploaded via UploadToURL to get a public URL, then used to create Instagram child media containers. All slides are combined into a carousel, published, and a Slack notification is sent.

Flow:
Webhook → Validate → Build caption → Process slides → Upload images → Create IG containers → Publish carousel → Notify Slack

Required:
IG_USER_ID, IG_ACCESS_TOKEN, SLACK_CHANNEL_ID

Payload:
collectionName, caption, hook, cta, hashtags[],
slides[]: { imageUrl, title, price, currency }

Webhook – Receive Payload: Accepts POST requests to /ig-carousel and provides an inline response upon workflow completion.

Code – Validate: Verifies that the payload contains 2–10 valid HTTPS image URLs and required environment variables, failing fast with descriptive errors if criteria aren't met.

Code – Build Caption: Construct a structured Instagram caption including hooks, product lists, and CTAs, ensuring a safe limit of 2,200 characters.

Split In Batches: Iterates through the slides array one by one to trigger individual image processing, then routes to assembly once all items are processed.

HTTP – Fetch Slide Image: Downloads each slide's image as a binary file to prepare it for hosting.

Upload to URL: Uploads the binary to a CDN to generate a mandatory public HTTPS URL, which is required by Instagram's API for carousel items.

Code – Create Child Container: Communicates with the Instagram Graph API to generate a specific "child" media container marked as a carousel item, returning its unique ID.

Code – Aggregate Child IDs: Collects all individual child container IDs from the loop and joins them into a comma-separated string required for the parent API call. It also re-attaches the final caption and metadata via cross-node references.

HTTP – Create Carousel Container: Submits a POST request to the Instagram Graph API to create a parent container with the media_type set to CAROUSEL. The caption is applied exclusively to this parent container.

Wait 8s: Pauses the workflow for 8 seconds to allow Instagram sufficient time to validate and process the multiple assets within the carousel.

Nodes 11-14: Publish, Metadata, Notify, Respond

HTTP – Publish Carousel: Finalizes the process by calling the /media_publish endpoint with the parent container ID, returning the official live Instagram Post ID.

HTTP – Fetch Post Metadata: Automatically retrieves the live permalink, timestamp, and media type to provide a direct URL for the published content.

Slack – Notify Team: Dispatches a formatted alert to Slack containing the collection name, slide count, and a direct link to the new post.

Respond to Webhook: Concludes the workflow by returning a JSON success payload to the initial caller, including the media ID, permalink, and slide count.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, mediaId: $('IG Publish Carousel1').item.json.id, permalink: $('HTTP Fetch Post Metadata1').item.json.permalink, slideCount: $('Code Aggregate Child IDs1').item.json.slideCount, collectionName: $('Code Aggregate Child IDs1').item.json.collectionName }) }}
